creator: bugzilla.freebsd@omnilan.de
text:
I haven't followed the changes in the isc-dhcpd port for quite some time, since I use jails with a dedicated (ng_bridge(4)ed) interface for security reasons, so I'm not affected anymore.

The latest patch looks like it does what every previous patch has done: rename "dhcpd_rootdir" to "dhcpd_chrootdir".

Can someone please incorporate that change, after several years of misbehaving paranoid/chroot feature?
It's such a simple convention, originating in base and simply following it provides a clean and easy way to get syslog working.  I can't understand why this is still an issue after such a long time.

-harry
